title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
code: # Install packages if needed # install.packages(c("survival", "purrr", "ggplot2", "gt", "tibble")) # install.packages("pak") # pak::pak("zabore/ipcw")  library(ipcw) library(survival) library(ggplot2) library(purrr) library(gt) library(tibble)

what: Simulate data and compute IPCW weights
title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
text: First, create simulated dataset n=500 patients using sim_data_cr() function. use baseline-dependent censoring censoring informative, keep parameters besides n default values: Key columns simulated data include: z1: baseline PSA quartile (0 = lowest, 3 = highest) delta: event indicator (1 = metastasis, 2 = death, 0 = censored) t: observed time (event censoring) wide_to_long_cr() converts data long format, required IPCW estimation. add_ipcw_weights_cr() function computes IPCW weights competing risks data. strat argument specifies whether use stratified (non-parametric) weights (strat = "yes") Cox model weights (strat = "").
code: set.seed(9843)  dat <-    sim_data_cr(     n = 500,     censoring = "baseline",     beta1 = log(1.5),     beta2 = log(2.25),     beta3 = log(3.4),     p = 0.3     ) dat_long <- wide_to_long_cr(dat)  # Cox model weights dat_long_cox <- add_ipcw_weights_cr(dat_long, strat = "no")  # Stratified (non-parametric) weights dat_long_strat <- add_ipcw_weights_cr(dat_long, strat = "yes")

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
text: two sets weights result nearly identical weighted cumulative incidence curves example.
code: # Get an ordered vector of times esttimes <- sort(dat$t)  # Estimate cumulative incidence using the stratified (non-parametric) IPCW ci_strat <- cuminc_waverage_cr(dat, esttimes)  # Estimate cumulative incidence using the Cox model IPCW ci_cox <- cuminc_ipcw_cr(dat_long_cox, esttimes) # Combine the two sets of weights for plotting to_plot <-    rbind(     data.frame(time = esttimes, est = ci_strat, method = "Weighted average"),     data.frame(time = esttimes, est = ci_cox,  method = "Cox IPCW")     )  ggplot(to_plot, aes(x = time, y = est,                      color = method, linetype = method, linewidth = method)) +   geom_step() +   scale_color_manual(values = c("#f08122", "#5c2161")) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "dashed")) +   scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(0.8, 1.1)) +   xlim(0, 5) +    ylim(0, 1) +   labs(     x = "Time",     y = "Cumulative incidence of metastasis"   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(     legend.position = "bottom",      legend.title = element_blank()     ) #> Warning: Removed 52 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_step()`).

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
text: Next, estimate subdistribution hazard ratio (SHR) association baseline PSA metastasis, accounting competing risk death dependent censoring. fg_split_cr() function splits data event-specific datasets, add_fg_weights_cr() function computes IPCW weights Fine-Gray regression. fg_weighted_cr() function fits weighted Fine-Gray model.
code: # Split the data into event-specific datasets for Fine-Gray regression dat_long_fg <- fg_split_cr(dat_long)  # Stratified weights dat_long_fg_strat <- add_fg_weights_cr(dat_long_fg, strat = "yes") fg_strat <- fg_weighted_cr(dat_long_fg_strat, extend = FALSE) exp(fg_strat[, 1])   # exponentiated coefficients #>      z11      z12      z13  #> 1.707717 2.529884 3.168057  # Cox model weights dat_long_fg_cox <- add_fg_weights_cr(dat_long_fg, strat = "no") fg_cox <- fg_weighted_cr(dat_long_fg_cox) exp(fg_cox[, 1]) #>      z11      z12      z13  #> 1.747758 2.574544 3.124305  # Table of results tibble(     Method = rep(c("Fine-Gray stratified", "Fine-Gray Cox"), each = 3),     Effect = rep(c("PSA Q2 vs Q1", "PSA Q3 vs Q1", "PSA Q4 vs Q1"), 2),     Estimate = c(exp(fg_strat[, 1]), exp(fg_cox[, 1]))   ) |>    gt() |>    fmt_number(columns = "Estimate", decimals = 2) |>   tab_header("IPCW Fine-gray regression results for the association between PSA and metastasis, using both stratified and Cox IPCW weights")

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
text: Bootstrap resampling provides valid standard errors weights estimated. get_ipcw_boot_cr() draws bootstrap samples wide-format data, converts long format, appends IPCW weights one step, mirroring get_ipcw_boot_se() single-event case. strat argument controls weight type, matching add_ipcw_weights_cr(). process takes time, resulting object fg_strat_boot included package. get_boot_pci_cr() computes bootstrap percentile interval column matrix bootstrap estimates, mirroring get_boot_pci_se() single-event case. bootstrap logic can generalized competing risks quantity returns vector matrix estimates. example, plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr() combines bootstrap samples get_ipcw_boot_cr() Cox IPCW cumulative incidence estimate produce cumulative incidence plot bootstrap percentile confidence intervals, mirroring plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se() single-event case. Bootstrapping data generating pointwise confidence intervals time consuming, code evaluated . following plot IPCW weighted cumulative incidence metastatis, using Cox model weights, bootstrap confidence intervals.
code: # Bootstrap samples with Cox IPCW weights boot_dat_long <- get_ipcw_boot_cr(dat, B = 500, strat = "no", seed = 20240917)  # Fit the stratified Fine-Gray model to each bootstrap sample fg_strat_boot <-    map(     boot_dat_long,      ~ fg_weighted_cr(       add_fg_weights_cr(fg_split_cr(.), strat = "yes"), extend = FALSE)[, 1]     )  fg_strat_boot <- matrix(unlist(fg_strat_boot), ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE) pci_fg_strat <- get_boot_pci_cr(fg_strat_boot)   result_tab <- data.frame(   Effect = c("PSA Q2 vs Q1", "PSA Q3 vs Q1", "PSA Q4 vs Q1"),   Estimate = exp(fg_strat[, 1]),   Lower_95pct_PI = exp(pci_fg_strat["lower", ]),   Upper_95pct_PI = exp(pci_fg_strat["upper", ]) )  result_tab |>    gt() |>    fmt_number(     columns = c("Estimate", "Lower_95pct_PI", "Upper_95pct_PI"),      decimals = 2) |>    cols_label(     Lower_95pct_PI = "Lower 95% PI",     Upper_95pct_PI = "Upper 95% PI"   ) |>    # Add a footnote   tab_footnote(     footnote = "PI = percentile interval",     locations = cells_column_labels(columns = c(Lower_95pct_PI, Upper_95pct_PI))   ) |>    tab_header(     "IPCW Fine-Gray regression results for the association between PSA quartile and metastasis, using stratified IPCW weights"   ) boot_dat_long <-    get_ipcw_boot_cr(     dat,      B = 500,      strat = "no",      seed = 20240917)  plot_base <-    plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr(     boot_dat_long,      dat_long_cox,      esttimes = esttimes)  plot_base +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 6, 1)) +    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +   labs(     x = "Time",     y = "Cumulative incidence of metastasis"   )

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
code: # Install packages if needed # install.packages(c(""survival", "purrr", "ggsurvfit", "gt")) # install.packages("pak") # pak::pak("zabore/ipcw")  library(ipcw) library(survival) library(purrr) library(ggsurvfit) library(gt)

what: Simulate data and compute IPCW weights
title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: First, create simulated dataset n=500 patients using sim_data_se() function arguments set defaults: Key columns simulated data include: t: observed time (event censoring) delta: event indicator (1 = event, 0 = censored) x: treatment (0 = chemotherapy, 1 = TKI) W2: ancillary biomarker drives informative censoring (ANC) get_ipcw_wgt_se() converts wide dataset counting-process (long) format appends unstabilized IPCW weight column wgt.
code: set.seed(67) dat <-    sim_data_se(     n = 500,     alpha = 0.05,     x_prop = 0.5,     a = 2,     sigma = 500,     beta = log(0.25),     lambda = 0.01,     phi = -5     ) dat_long <- get_ipcw_wgt_se(dat)

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: First, get appropriate confidence interval Kaplan-Meier plot, need account fact IPCW estimated data. using bootstrapping produce Kaplan-Meier plot bootstrap-based pointwise confidence intervals. First, generate bootstrapped datasets weights using function get_ipcw_boot_se(). Note depending size data number bootstrap samples, bootstrapping step can take time run, code executed . , create plot plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se() function. can take time due large size boostrapped data calculation pointwise confidence intervals, code executed .  Patients TKI group higher progression-free survival probability chemotherapy group, bootstrap-based confidence intervals overlapping follow-period.
code: boot_dat <-    get_ipcw_boot_se(     data = dat,      B = 500,      time_var = "t",      event_var = "delta",     cens_cov = "W2",      seed = 20240917) plot_base <-   plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se(     boot_data = boot_dat,      orig_data = dat_long,      pre_times = seq(0, 2429, 1),     covariate = "x",     weight_var = "wgt",     event_var = "delta")  plot_base +   xlim(c(0, 1000)) +   scale_color_manual(labels = c("Chemotherapy", "TKI"),                         values = c("#0078bf", "#f7c612")) +   scale_fill_manual(labels = c("Chemotherapy", "TKI"),                                   values = c("#0078bf", "#f7c612")) +   labs(     color = "",     fill = "",     x = "Days from treatment start",     y = "Progression-free survival probability"   ) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: Next, estimate hazard ratio (HR) association treatment group PFS, accounting dependent censoring. Use convenience wrapper get_ipcw_cox_fit_se() obtain tibble results weighted Cox regression model. fit Cox model bootstrap datasets extract log(HR) model fit. used construct bootstrap percentile intervals. process takes time, resulting object cox_boot_lhr included package. Use function get_boot_pci_se() dataframe log(HR)s obtain bootstrap-based 95% confidence interval. combine results table:
code: cox_fit <- get_ipcw_cox_fit_se(dat_long, weight = "wgt")  cox_fit #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   term  log_hr log_hr_se log_hr_rob_se    hr hr_ci_low hr_ci_high #>   <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 x      -1.41     0.109         0.183 0.243     0.170      0.348 # Fit the Cox model to the B bootstrap samples cox_boot_fit <-   boot_dat |>   map(~ get_ipcw_cox_fit_se(., covariate = "x", weight = "wgt"))  # Extract the B log HRs  cox_boot_lhr <-    map_dbl(cox_boot_fit, "log_hr") # Calculate the bootstrap percentile interval boot_pci <- get_boot_pci_se(data.frame(log_hr = cox_boot_lhr)) result_tab <- data.frame(   Scale = c("log(HR)", "HR"),   Estimate = round(c(cox_fit$log_hr, exp(cox_fit$log_hr)), 2),   Lower_95pct_PI = round(c(boot_pci[[1]], exp(boot_pci[[1]])), 2),   Upper_95pct_PI = round(c(boot_pci[[2]], exp(boot_pci[[2]])), 2) )  result_tab |>    gt() |>    cols_label(     Scale = "Scale",     Estimate = "Estimate",     Lower_95pct_PI = "Lower 95% PI",     Upper_95pct_PI = "Upper 95% PI"   ) |>    # Add a footnote   tab_footnote(     footnote = "PI = percentile interval",     locations = cells_column_labels(columns = c(Lower_95pct_PI, Upper_95pct_PI))   ) |>    tab_header(     "IPCW Cox regression results for the association between treatment group and progression-free survival"   )

what: ipcw: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighted Methods
title: ipcw: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighted Methods
text: ipcw package estimates inverse probability censoring weights (IPCW) right-censored data either single event competing events.

title: ipcw: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighted Methods
text: can install development version ipcw GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("zabore/ipcw")

title: ipcw: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighted Methods
text: Dependent censoring occurs participants censored point time , less, likely event going forward compared participants remain study. leads distribution residual time event interest different censored uncensored populations. Dependent censoring can bias standard survival estimates. examples use ipcw package’s simulated example datasets compare unadjusted estimates IPCW-adjusted estimates, single event competing risks.
code: # Install packages if needed # install.packages(c(""survival", "ggsurvfit", "dplyr")) # install.packages("pak") # pak::pak("zabore/ipcw")  # Load packages library(ipcw) library(survival) library(ggsurvfit) library(dplyr)

title: ipcw: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighted Methods
text: example single event type, one covariate interest, x.  Note plot demonstration purposes . practice, dependent censoring present IPCW-adjusted curves interest. example, see unadjusted estimates -estimate survival probability groups, adjustment dependent censoring corrects . See vignette("single-event-guided-example") thorough demonstration methods implemented package single event settings, including bootstrap confidence intervals IPCW-adjusted Kaplan-Meier curves Cox regression.
code: set.seed(67)  dat <- sim_data_se(n = 500)  # Unadjusted (naive) Kaplan-Meier, ignoring dependent censoring naive_fit <-    survfit(     Surv(t, delta) ~ x,      data = dat)  # Convert to counting-process (long) format and compute IPCW dat_long <- get_ipcw_wgt_se(dat)  # IPCW-adjusted Kaplan-Meier ipcw_fit <-    survfit(     Surv(tstart, tstop, delta) ~ x,      data = dat_long,     weights = wgt,      timefix = FALSE)

title: ipcw: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighted Methods
text: example, see unadjusted estimates overestimate cumulative incidence event 1 presence competing risk event 2. Adjustment dependent censoring weighting corrects . See vignette("competing-risks-guided-example") thorough demonstration methods implemented package competing risks settings, including Fine-Gray regression bootstrap standard errors.
code: set.seed(9843)  dat_cr <- sim_data_cr(n = 500, censoring = "baseline")  esttimes <- seq(0, 5, 0.1)  # Unadjusted (naive) cumulative incidence, ignoring dependent censoring ci_naive <- cuminc_naive_cr(dat_cr, esttimes)  # Convert to counting-process (long) format and compute IPCW dat_long_cr <- add_ipcw_weights_cr(wide_to_long_cr(dat_cr), strat = "no")  # IPCW-adjusted cumulative incidence ci_ipcw     <- cuminc_ipcw_cr(dat_long_cr, esttimes)

what: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights_cr
title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights_cr
text: Computes probability remaining uncensored type-2 event appends column p_notcens_after_death.

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights_cr
code: add_fg_weights_cr(data_long_fg, covariate = "z1", strat = "no")

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights_cr
text: data_long_fg data frame Fine-Gray format, returned fg_split_cr(). covariate Character string. Name covariate column. Default "z1". strat Character. Passed add_ipcw_weights_cr(). "" (default) uses Cox model; "yes" uses stratum-specific KM estimates.

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights_cr
text: data_long_fg additional column p_notcens_after_death.

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long    <- wide_to_long_cr(dat) dat_long_fg <- fg_split_cr(dat_long) dat_long_fg <- add_fg_weights_cr(dat_long_fg, strat = "no") summary(dat_long_fg$p_notcens_after_death) #>      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max.  #> 0.0002632 1.0000000 1.0000000 0.9525524 1.0000000 1.0000000

what: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights_cr
title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights_cr
text: Estimates probability remaining uncensored appends column p_notcens dataset. Supports two estimation strategies: Cox proportional hazards model (strat = "") non-parametric (KM) estimates within level covariate (strat = "yes").

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights_cr
code: add_ipcw_weights_cr(   data_long,   covariate = "z1",   strat = "no",   new_data = NULL,   by.start = TRUE )

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights_cr
text: data_long data frame long format, returned wide_to_long_cr(). Must contain columns tstart, tstop, censor, covariate named covariate. covariate Character string. Name covariate column used model censoring distribution. Default "z1". strat Character. "" (default) fits single Cox model censoring distribution using covariate. "yes" estimates censoring distribution non-parametrically within stratum covariate. new_data Optional data frame weights applied. NULL (default), weights computed data_long . .start Logical. TRUE (default), weight P(censored tstart). FALSE, weight P(censored interval tstart tstop).

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights_cr
text: new_data (data_long new_data NULL) additional column p_notcens.

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_cr(dat) dat_long <- add_ipcw_weights_cr(dat_long, strat = "no") summary(dat_long$p_notcens) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>  0.0547  0.7641  0.9126  0.8413  0.9757  1.0000

what: Bootstrap log hazard ratios from IPCW Cox models — cox_boot_lhr
title: Bootstrap log hazard ratios from IPCW Cox models — cox_boot_lhr
text: numeric vector 500 bootstrap log hazard ratio estimates effect treatment (x) progression-free survival, obtained fitting IPCW-weighted Cox model one bootstrap sample single_example_dat. Provided package data avoid re-running time-consuming bootstrap vignettes examples. Use get_boot_pci_se() get_boot_var_se() obtain bootstrap-based confidence intervals variance estimates.

title: Bootstrap log hazard ratios from IPCW Cox models — cox_boot_lhr
code: data(cox_boot_lhr)

title: Bootstrap log hazard ratios from IPCW Cox models — cox_boot_lhr
text: numeric vector length 500.

title: Bootstrap log hazard ratios from IPCW Cox models — cox_boot_lhr
text: Generated authors applying get_ipcw_boot_se() single_example_dat B = 500 seed = 20240917, fitting get_ipcw_cox_fit_se() bootstrap sample extracting log_hr.

what: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw_cr
title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw_cr
text: Estimates marginal cumulative incidence event type 1 using weighted Aalen-Johansen estimator, weights IPCW weights stored p_notcens column (added add_ipcw_weights_cr()).

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw_cr
code: cuminc_ipcw_cr(   data_long,   esttimes = seq(from = 0, to = 10, length.out = 100),   extend = TRUE,   covariate = "z1" )

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw_cr
text: data_long long-format data frame IPCW weights, returned add_ipcw_weights_cr(). Must contain columns tstart, tstop, delta, id, p_notcens. esttimes Numeric vector times return estimates. Defaults 100 equally spaced points 0 10. extend Logical. FALSE, estimates beyond minimum stratum-specific maximum follow-times set NA. Default TRUE. covariate Character string. Name covariate column, used extend = FALSE compute truncation time. Default "z1".

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw_cr
text: numeric vector IPCW cumulative incidence estimates esttimes.

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 200, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_cr(dat) dat_long <- add_ipcw_weights_cr(dat_long, strat = "no") #> Warning: Loglik converged before variable  3 ; beta may be infinite.  cuminc_ipcw_cr(dat_long, esttimes = seq(0, 5, 0.5)) #>  [1] 0.0000000 0.2525084 0.3656652 0.4349979 0.4489364 0.4768644 0.4768644 #>  [8] 0.4768644 0.4847719 0.4847719 0.4847719

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive_cr
text: Estimates cumulative incidence event type 1 using standard Aalen-Johansen estimator without IPCW adjustment. Serves comparison method censoring independent covariates.

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive_cr
code: cuminc_naive_cr(dat, esttimes, time_var = "t", event_var = "delta")

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive_cr
text: dat wide-format competing risks data frame containing columns specified time_var event_var. esttimes Numeric vector times return estimates. time_var Character string. Name event/censoring time column. Default "t". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column. Default "delta".

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive_cr
text: numeric vector cumulative incidence estimates esttimes.

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive_cr

what: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage_cr
title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage_cr
text: Estimates marginal cumulative incidence event type 1 computing stratum-specific Aalen-Johansen estimates combining sample-proportion weights. non-parametric IPCW approach.

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage_cr
code: cuminc_waverage_cr(   dat,   esttimes = seq(from = 0, to = 10, length.out = 100),   time_var = "t",   event_var = "delta",   covariate = "z1" )

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage_cr
text: dat wide-format competing risks data frame containing columns specified time_var, event_var, covariate. esttimes Numeric vector times return estimates. Defaults 100 equally spaced points 0 10. time_var Character string. Name event/censoring time column. Default "t". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column. Default "delta". covariate Character string. Name stratification covariate column. Default "z1".

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage_cr
text: numeric vector weighted-average cumulative incidence estimates esttimes. Values beyond minimum stratum-specific maximum follow-times set NA.

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage_cr

what: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive_cr
title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive_cr
text: Fits Fine-Gray model using standard survival::finegray() approach, without IPCW adjustment. Serves comparison fg_weighted_cr().

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive_cr
code: fg_naive_cr(dat, time_var = "t", event_var = "delta", covariate = "z1")

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive_cr
text: dat wide-format competing risks data frame containing columns specified time_var, event_var, covariate. time_var Character string. Name event/censoring time column. Default "t". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column. Default "delta". covariate Character string. Name covariate column. Default "z1".

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive_cr
text: matrix one row per term two columns: log sub-distribution hazard ratio standard error.

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 200, censoring = "independent") fg_naive_cr(dat) #>          coef  se(coef) #> z11 0.9452312 0.3624174 #> z12 1.3652479 0.3799441 #> z13 1.7795374 0.3446775

what: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split_cr
title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split_cr
text: type-2 event, subjects artificially re-entered risk set (Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard model). function appends additional rows, split every censoring time.

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split_cr
code: fg_split_cr(data_long, covariate = "z1", event2_level = "event_2")

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split_cr
text: data_long long-format data frame, returned wide_to_long_cr(). covariate Character string. Name covariate column. Default "z1". event2_level Character string. Factor level delta column representing competing event. Default "event_2".

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split_cr
text: data frame Fine-Gray format, additional rows subjects experienced competing event, sorted id tstart.

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_cr(dat) dat_long_fg <- fg_split_cr(dat_long) nrow(dat_long_fg) > nrow(dat_long) #> [1] TRUE

what: Bootstrap Fine-Gray coefficients from stratified IPCW weights — fg_strat_boot
title: Bootstrap Fine-Gray coefficients from stratified IPCW weights — fg_strat_boot
text: 500--3 matrix bootstrap log sub-distribution hazard ratio estimates effect baseline PSA quartile (z1) metastasis, row obtained fitting stratified-weight Fine-Gray model one bootstrap sample competing risks guided example data. Provided package data avoid re-running time-consuming bootstrap vignettes examples. Use get_boot_pci_cr() obtain bootstrap-based confidence intervals.

title: Bootstrap Fine-Gray coefficients from stratified IPCW weights — fg_strat_boot
code: data(fg_strat_boot)

title: Bootstrap Fine-Gray coefficients from stratified IPCW weights — fg_strat_boot
text: numeric matrix 500 rows (bootstrap replicates) 3 columns (z1 levels 1, 2, 3 vs. reference level 0).

title: Bootstrap Fine-Gray coefficients from stratified IPCW weights — fg_strat_boot
text: Generated authors applying get_ipcw_boot_cr() B = 500 seed = 20240917 competing risks dataset simulated via sim_data_cr(n = 500, censoring = "baseline", beta1 = log(1.5), beta2 = log(2.25), beta3 = log(3.4), p = 0.3) set.seed(9843), fitting fg_weighted_cr() stratified weights (add_fg_weights_cr() strat = "yes") bootstrap sample extracting coefficient column.

what: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted_cr
title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted_cr
text: Fits Fine-Gray model Fine-Gray split dataset, weighting inverse probability remaining uncensored competing event (p_notcens_after_death), uses robust sandwich variance via cluster(id).

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted_cr
code: fg_weighted_cr(   data_long_fg,   covariate = "z1",   extend = TRUE,   event1_level = "event_1" )

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted_cr
text: data_long_fg data frame Fine-Gray format weights, returned add_fg_weights_cr(). covariate Character string. Name covariate column. Default "z1". extend Logical. FALSE, data truncated minimum stratum-specific maximum follow-times fitting. Default TRUE. event1_level Character string. Factor level delta column representing primary event. Default "event_1".

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted_cr
text: matrix one row per term two columns: log sub-distribution hazard ratio robust standard error.

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 300, censoring = "baseline") dat_long    <- wide_to_long_cr(dat) dat_long_fg <- fg_split_cr(dat_long) dat_long_fg <- add_fg_weights_cr(dat_long_fg, strat = "no") fg_weighted_cr(dat_long_fg) #>          coef robust se #> z11 0.9135669 0.3975405 #> z12 1.3004739 0.3955882 #> z13 1.4468114 0.3928682

what: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence intervals for competing risks estimates — get_boot_pci_cr
title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence intervals for competing risks estimates — get_boot_pci_cr
text: Computes 2.5th 97.5th percentiles bootstrap distribution column matrix bootstrap estimates. Intended competing risks quantities (e.g. cumulative incidence set time points, Fine-Gray regression terms), bootstrap replicate produces one estimate, unlike single scalar log hazard ratio handled get_boot_pci_se().

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence intervals for competing risks estimates — get_boot_pci_cr
code: get_boot_pci_cr(boot_mat)

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence intervals for competing risks estimates — get_boot_pci_cr
text: boot_mat numeric matrix bootstrap estimates, one row per bootstrap replicate one column per estimand (e.g. time point regression term), returned row-binding results multiple calls cuminc_waverage_cr(), cuminc_ipcw_cr(), fg_weighted_cr().

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence intervals for competing risks estimates — get_boot_pci_cr
text: 2-row numeric matrix rows "lower" "upper" giving 2.5th 97.5th percentiles bootstrap distribution column boot_mat. Columns NA bootstrap estimate return NA bounds.

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence intervals for competing risks estimates — get_boot_pci_cr

what: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci_se
title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci_se
text: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval log hazard ratio

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci_se
code: get_boot_pci_se(data)

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci_se
text: data data frame column log_hr containing bootstrap log hazard ratio estimates, returned multiple calls get_ipcw_cox_fit_se() get_cox_fit_se().

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci_se
text: named numeric vector length 2 giving 2.5th 97.5th percentiles bootstrap distribution.

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci_se
code: boot_results <- data.frame(log_hr = rnorm(500, mean = -0.5, sd = 0.2)) get_boot_pci_se(boot_results) #>        2.5%       97.5%  #> -0.92922493 -0.09525084

what: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var_se
title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var_se
text: Compute bootstrap variance log hazard ratio

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var_se
code: get_boot_var_se(data, B)

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var_se
text: data data frame column log_hr containing bootstrap log hazard ratio estimates, returned multiple calls get_ipcw_cox_fit_se() get_cox_fit_se(). B Integer. Number bootstrap samples used.

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var_se
text: single numeric value: bootstrap variance estimate.

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var_se
code: # Toy example with 10 simulated bootstrap log HRs boot_results <- data.frame(log_hr = rnorm(10, mean = -0.5, sd = 0.2)) get_boot_var_se(boot_results, B = 10) #> [1] 0.05129527

what: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit_se
title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit_se
text: Convenience wrapper around survival::coxph() using counting-process (tstart, tstop) time variables. Returns log-hazard ratio estimates standard errors hazard ratio 95% confidence intervals.

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit_se
code: get_cox_fit_se(data, covariate = "x")

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit_se
text: data data frame long (counting-process) format containing columns tstart, tstop, delta, covariate named covariate. covariate Character string. Name predictor covariate column. Default "x".

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit_se
text: data frame one row per term containing: term Covariate name. log_hr Log hazard ratio estimate. log_hr_se Standard error log hazard ratio. hr Hazard ratio estimate. hr_ci_low Lower 95% confidence limit. hr_ci_high Upper 95% confidence limit.

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit_se
code: set.seed(20240429) dat <- sim_data_se(n = 500) dat_long <- get_ipcw_wgt_se(dat) get_cox_fit_se(dat_long) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   term  log_hr log_hr_se    hr hr_ci_low hr_ci_high #>   <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 x      -1.85     0.147 0.157     0.117      0.209

what: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for competing risks survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_cr
title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for competing risks survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_cr
text: Draws B bootstrap samples original wide-format competing risks data, converts sample long (counting-process) format via wide_to_long_cr(), appends IPCW weights via add_ipcw_weights_cr(). Returns results list long-format data frames can used directly cuminc_ipcw_cr(), processed fg_split_cr() add_fg_weights_cr() Fine-Gray bootstrap.

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for competing risks survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_cr
code: get_ipcw_boot_cr(   data,   B = 500,   time_var = "t",   event_var = "delta",   covariate = "z1",   cens_level = "censor",   event2_level = "event_2",   strat = "no",   seed = NULL )

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for competing risks survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_cr
text: data wide-format competing risks data frame containing columns specified time_var, event_var, covariate. B Integer. Number bootstrap samples. Default 500. time_var Character string. Name event/censoring time column. Default "t". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column. Default "delta". covariate Character string. Name covariate column. Default "z1". cens_level Character string. Factor level event_var representing censoring. Default "censor". event2_level Character string. Factor level event_var representing competing event (event type 2). Default "event_2". strat Character. Passed add_ipcw_weights_cr(). "" (default) fits single Cox model censoring distribution; "yes" estimates censoring distribution non-parametrically within stratum covariate. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. Default NULL.

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for competing risks survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_cr
text: list B data frames, long (counting-process) format IPCW weight column p_notcens, returned add_ipcw_weights_cr().

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for competing risks survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_cr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 200, censoring = "baseline") boot_list <- get_ipcw_boot_cr(dat, B = 50) } # }

what: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for single-event survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_se
title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for single-event survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_se
text: Draws B bootstrap samples original wide-format data, fits IPCW weights sample via get_ipcw_wgt_se(), returns results list long-format data frames. list can passed plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se() plotting used directly bootstrap quantities (e.g. Cox hazard ratios via get_ipcw_cox_fit_se()).

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for single-event survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_se
code: get_ipcw_boot_se(   data,   B = 500,   time_var = "t",   event_var = "delta",   cens_cov = "W2",   seed = NULL )

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for single-event survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_se
text: data wide-format data frame containing columns specified time_var, event_var, cens_cov. B Integer. Number bootstrap samples. Default 500. time_var Character string. Name observed follow-time column. Default "t". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column (1 = event, 0 = censored). Default "delta". cens_cov Character string. Name covariate column used model censoring distribution. Default "W2". seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. Default NULL.

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for single-event survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_se
text: list B data frames, long (counting-process) format returned get_ipcw_wgt_se().

title: Bootstrap IPCW weighted data for single-event survival analysis — get_ipcw_boot_se
code: # Toy example with only 10 bootstrap samples set.seed(1) dat <- sim_data_se(n = 200) boot_list <- get_ipcw_boot_se(dat, B = 10)

what: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit_se
title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit_se
text: Fits weighted Cox model robust sandwich variance returns log-hazard ratio estimates together model-based robust standard errors, plus hazard ratio 95% confidence interval based robust standard error. Convenience wrapper survival::coxph().

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit_se
code: get_ipcw_cox_fit_se(data, covariate = "x", weight = "wgt")

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit_se
text: data data frame long (counting-process) format, returned get_ipcw_wgt_se(). Must contain columns tstart, tstop, delta, id, covariate named covariate, weight column named weight. covariate Character string. Name predictor covariate column. Default "x". weight Character string. Name weight column. Default "wgt".

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit_se
text: data frame one row per term containing: term Covariate name. log_hr Log hazard ratio estimate. log_hr_se Model-based standard error log hazard ratio. log_hr_rob_se Robust (sandwich) standard error log hazard ratio. hr Hazard ratio estimate. hr_ci_low Lower 95% confidence limit (based robust SE). hr_ci_high Upper 95% confidence limit (based robust SE).

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit_se
code: set.seed(20240429) dat <- sim_data_se(n = 500) dat_long <- get_ipcw_wgt_se(dat) get_ipcw_cox_fit_se(dat_long, weight = "wgt") #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   term  log_hr log_hr_se log_hr_rob_se    hr hr_ci_low hr_ci_high #>   <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 x      -1.21     0.103         0.162 0.298     0.217      0.410

what: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se
title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se
text: Fits weighted Kaplan-Meier estimator stratified specified covariate returns survival probabilities evaluated pre-specified set times.

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se
code: get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se(   data,   covariate = "x",   weight_var = "wgt",   event_var = "delta",   pre_times = seq(0, 50, 1) )

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se
text: data data frame long (counting-process) format IPCW weight column, returned get_ipcw_wgt_se(). Must contain time columns named tstart tstop counting process time intervals. covariate Character string. Name stratification covariate column. Default "x". weight_var Character string. Name IPCW weight column. Default "wgt". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column (1 = event, 0 = censored). Default "delta". pre_times Numeric vector times evaluate survival probabilities. Defaults seq(0, 50, 1). Choose second number cover entire range times observed data

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se
text: tibble columns time, surv (survival probability), column named covariate containing stratum labels.

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se
code: set.seed(20240429) dat <- sim_data_se(n = 500) dat_long <- get_ipcw_wgt_se(dat) get_ipcw_km_prob_x_se(dat_long, pre_times = seq(0, 2429, 1)) #> # A tibble: 3,724 × 3 #>     time  surv x     #>    <dbl> <dbl> <fct> #>  1     0     1 x=0   #>  2     1     1 x=0   #>  3     2     1 x=0   #>  4     3     1 x=0   #>  5     4     1 x=0   #>  6     5     1 x=0   #>  7     6     1 x=0   #>  8     7     1 x=0   #>  9     8     1 x=0   #> 10     9     1 x=0   #> # ℹ 3,714 more rows

what: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt_se
title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt_se
text: Fits Cox model censoring distribution returns original dataset counting-process (long) format unstabilized IPCW weight column appended.

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt_se
code: get_ipcw_wgt_se(data, time_var = "t", event_var = "delta", cens_cov = "W2")

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt_se
text: data data frame containing follow-time, event indicator, censoring covariate columns. time_var Character string. Name observed follow-time column. Default "t". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column (1 = event, 0 = censored). Default "delta". cens_cov Character string. Name covariate column used Cox model censoring distribution. Default "W2".

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt_se
text: data frame long (counting-process) format columns tstart, tstop, delta (event indicator), id, wgt (unstabilized IPCW weight), original columns.

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt_se

what: Plot IPCW cumulative incidence with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr
title: Plot IPCW cumulative incidence with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr
text: Combines bootstrap cumulative incidence curves get_ipcw_boot_cr() point estimate original IPCW-weighted data produce cumulative incidence plot 95% percentile confidence intervals, mirroring plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se() single-event case.

title: Plot IPCW cumulative incidence with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr
code: plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr(   boot_data,   orig_data,   esttimes = seq(from = 0, to = 10, length.out = 100),   extend = TRUE,   covariate = "z1" )

title: Plot IPCW cumulative incidence with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr
text: boot_data list long-format data frames IPCW weights, returned get_ipcw_boot_cr(). orig_data single long-format data frame IPCW weights original (non-bootstrapped) dataset, returned add_ipcw_weights_cr(). Used point estimate. esttimes Numeric vector times evaluate cumulative incidence. Defaults 100 equally spaced points 0 10. extend Logical. Passed cuminc_ipcw_cr(). Default TRUE. covariate Character string. Name covariate column, used extend = FALSE compute truncation time. Default "z1".

title: Plot IPCW cumulative incidence with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object. Add layers themes customise.

title: Plot IPCW cumulative incidence with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 200, censoring = "baseline") dat_long  <- add_ipcw_weights_cr(wide_to_long_cr(dat), strat = "no") boot_list <- get_ipcw_boot_cr(dat, B = 50) plot_ipcw_cuminc_boot_ci_cr(boot_list, dat_long, esttimes = seq(0, 5, 0.1)) } # }

what: Plot IPCW Kaplan-Meier curves with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se
title: Plot IPCW Kaplan-Meier curves with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se
text: Combines bootstrap survival curves get_ipcw_boot_se() point estimates original IPCW-weighted data produce Kaplan-Meier plot 95% percentile confidence intervals.

title: Plot IPCW Kaplan-Meier curves with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se
code: plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se(   boot_data,   orig_data,   pre_times = seq(0, 50, 1),   covariate = "x",   weight_var = "wgt",   event_var = "delta" )

title: Plot IPCW Kaplan-Meier curves with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se
text: boot_data list long-format data frames returned get_ipcw_boot_se(). orig_data single long-format data frame original (non-bootstrapped) dataset, returned get_ipcw_wgt_se(). Used point estimates. pre_times Numeric vector times evaluate survival probabilities. Default seq(0, 50, 1). covariate Character string. Name stratification covariate column. Default "x". weight_var Character string. Name IPCW weight column. Default "wgt". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column long-format data. Default "delta".

title: Plot IPCW Kaplan-Meier curves with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object. Add layers themes customise.

title: Plot IPCW Kaplan-Meier curves with bootstrap percentile confidence intervals — plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Not run due to computational time.  # Use a smaller n and/or smaller B for testing. set.seed(1) dat      <- sim_data_se(n = 500) dat_long <- get_ipcw_wgt_se(dat) boot_list <- get_ipcw_boot_se(dat, B = 500) plot_ipcw_km_boot_ci_se(boot_list, dat_long, pre_times = seq(0, 500, 10)) } # }

what: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_cr
title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_cr
text: Generates dataset two competing events sub-distribution hazard model parameterized four-level baseline covariate z1. Optionally adds independent covariate-dependent administrative censoring.

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_cr
code: sim_data_cr(   n = 100,   censoring = "none",   beta1 = log(1.5),   beta2 = log(2.25),   beta3 = log(3.4),   p = 0.3 )

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_cr
text: n Integer. Number subjects simulate. Default 100. censoring Character string specifying censoring mechanism. One "none", "independent", "baseline" (censoring rate depends z1). Default "none". beta1 Numeric. Log sub-distribution hazard ratio z1 = 1 vs z1 = 0 event type 1. Default log(1.5). beta2 Numeric. Log sub-distribution hazard ratio z1 = 2 vs z1 = 0 event type 1. Default log(2.25). beta3 Numeric. Log sub-distribution hazard ratio z1 = 3 vs z1 = 0 event type 1. Default log(3.4). p Numeric (0, 1). Baseline probability event type 1 reference group (z1 = 0). Default 0.3.

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_cr
text: data frame columns: z1 Four-level factor covariate (levels 0-3). delta Factor event indicator levels "censor", "event_1", "event_2". t Event censoring time.

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 200, censoring = "baseline") table(dat$delta) #>  #>  censor event_1 event_2  #>      74      85      41

what: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_se
title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_se
text: Generates dataset event times follow Weibull distribution parameterized binary treatment covariate x, censoring depends ancillary biomarker W2 shared gamma frailty, inducing informative censoring using Clayton's copula. data-generating mechanism used single-event guided example.

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_se
code: sim_data_se(   n = 500,   alpha = 0.05,   x_prop = 0.5,   a = 2,   sigma = 500,   beta = log(0.25),   lambda = 0.01,   phi = -5 )

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_se
text: n Integer. Number subjects simulate. Default 500. alpha Numeric. Shape parameter gamma frailty distribution, fixed rate 1. , alpha denotes level dependence survival time covariate drives censoring. smaller alpha leads stronger dependence. parameter also related Kendall's correlation coefficient, \(\tau\), \(\tau = (1/\alpha)/(1/\alpha + 2)\). Default 0.05. x_prop Numeric (0, 1). Probability treatment (x = 1). Default 0.5. Numeric. Weibull shape parameter event time distribution. Default 2. sigma Numeric. Weibull scale parameter event time distribution. Default 500. beta Numeric. Log hazard ratio treatment effect event time. Default log(0.25). lambda Numeric. Baseline censoring rate W2=0. Default 0.01. phi Numeric. log hazard ratio association one-unit increase W2 censoring time. Negative values mean higher W2 leads lower censoring rate (.e., longer follow-high-W2 subjects). Default -5.

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_se
text: data frame columns: S True (latent) event time. t Observed time (minimum S censoring time). delta Event indicator: 1 = event occurred, 0 = censored. x Binary treatment indicator (0 1). W2 Ancillary biomarker covariate drives informative censoring.

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_se
code: set.seed(20240429) dat <- sim_data_se(n = 500) table(dat$delta) #>  #>   0   1  #> 198 302  summary(dat$t) #>      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max.  #>    0.6884  172.7074  441.1759  552.9262  803.2563 2429.5429

what: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_cr
title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_cr
text: Splits wide dataset every censoring time, creating one rows per subject. resulting long dataset suitable fitting Cox models censoring distribution use add_ipcw_weights_cr().

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_cr
code: wide_to_long_cr(   dat,   time_var = "t",   event_var = "delta",   covariate = "z1",   cens_level = "censor",   event2_level = "event_2" )

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_cr
text: dat wide-format competing risks data frame. Must contain columns specified time_var, event_var, covariate. time_var Character string. Name event/censoring time column. Default "t". event_var Character string. Name event indicator column (factor levels censoring two event types). Default "delta". covariate Character string. Name covariate column. Default "z1". cens_level Character string. Factor level event_var representing censoring. Default "censor". event2_level Character string. Factor level event_var representing competing event (event type 2). Default "event_2".

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_cr
text: data frame long format columns id, delta, censor, event2_time, tstart, tstop, covariate column.

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_cr
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_cr(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_cr(dat) head(dat_long) #>   z1 id censor event2_time       tstart        tstop  delta #> 1  0  1      0          NA 0.0000000000 0.0008870787 censor #> 2  0  1      0          NA 0.0008870787 0.0236331037 censor #> 3  0  1      0          NA 0.0236331037 0.0295500713 censor #> 4  0  1      0          NA 0.0295500713 0.0724101320 censor #> 5  0  1      0          NA 0.0724101320 0.0812450008 censor #> 6  0  1      0          NA 0.0812450008 0.1824410379 censor

title: ipcw 0.1.0
text: Initial package version created
